frameworks.name: Value Equation for Affiliate Activation
frameworks.definition: Three levers for getting affiliates to act: reduce risk (testimonials, ongoing payments), increase speed (urgency window), and increase ease (pre-built emails, landing pages, CRM setup).

claims.text: Alex Hormozi asserts that when a B2B SaaS product has near-zero churn and six-figure LTV, an affiliate commission of $6,000-$10,000 per closed deal is economically justified, because a single customer generating $36,000-$60,000 in gross profit (at 55-60% gross margin on ~$100K LTV) makes paying $10K per referral an attractive 3-to-5x gross-profit-to-CAC ratio.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: B2B SaaS or service with near-zero churn
LTV in the six-figure range
gross margins 55-60%
claims.mechanism: When LTV is high and churn is near zero, the gross profit per customer far exceeds the affiliate payout, so the payout is a fraction of the value captured; under-compensating leaves activation money on the table.

numbers.axis: affiliate commission vs. gross profit per deal

anchors.region: gross profit per customer at 55-60% margin on ~$100K LTV

bounded_by: Does not apply to low-LTV or high-churn products where gross profit per customer may not exceed the upfront commission

claims.text: Alex Hormozi argues that low affiliate activation is primarily caused by insufficient commission size rather than non-financial friction: there is a threshold commission level below which money is not motivating, and above which it is; raising commission from $2K toward $6-10K crosses that threshold.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Affiliate commission currently set below the motivating threshold relative to the ask
claims.mechanism: Below-threshold commissions feel like noise and fail to override activation inertia; above-threshold commissions create sufficient economic motivation to overcome the friction of executing the first referral.
bounded_by: Abir notes that non-financial friction (activation complexity, social capital risk) may also play a role; Hormozi acknowledges this but prioritizes commission level as the primary lever

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends attaching a 30-day urgency window to the affiliate launch offer, explaining that affiliates who execute within the first 30 days have by far the most success, which provides a legitimate reason for the deadline rather than an artificial scarcity tactic.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Affiliate program where activation rate is low
Commission structure with a tiered or bonus option
claims.mechanism: Affiliates who act quickly have the most success because momentum is highest at launch; anchoring the bonus to a 30-day window creates urgency while the stated rationale (early activators succeed more) makes the deadline credible rather than manipulative.

claims.text: Alex Hormozi argues that offering ongoing (recurring) affiliate commissions substantially increases willingness to activate compared to one-time upfront spiffs, because affiliates perceive ongoing payments as lower risk and higher total value relative to a single payment.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Product with near-zero churn so the ongoing commission is credible and long-lived
Affiliate is a business owner who can model lifetime earnings from the stream
claims.mechanism: A one-time spiff requires the affiliate to bet social capital on a single payment; an ongoing percentage of recurring revenue means any individual referral that churns early is de-risked by the stream from others who stay, and total expected value is higher over the relationship.

anchors.value: 10-15% of recurring revenue per referred client

bounded_by: Only credible when the underlying product has near-zero churn; high-churn products make ongoing commissions feel like empty promises

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends providing white-labeled, pre-built marketing assets (email sequences, landing pages) and offering to send a team member to install them directly into the affiliate's CRM, because reducing the ease-of-activation barrier is one of the three levers (risk, speed, ease) that determines whether an affiliate converts their intent into their first referral.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Affiliate program where partners have agreed in principle but have not yet activated
Partners are small businesses or agencies without dedicated marketing teams
claims.mechanism: Affiliates who agree but delay face a hidden task list (build page, write emails, plug into CRM); pre-building and installing those assets eliminates the task list entirely, removing the primary operational reason for inaction.

claims.text: Alex Hormozi frames the value proposition for a white-labeled accounting/CFO service to the affiliate's end clients as making the affiliate's service appear free, by demonstrating that the accounting savings cover the cost of the affiliate's own product, turning the CFO service into a no-cost add-on from the client's perspective.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: White-labeled accounting or CFO service offered through an e-commerce agency affiliate
The accounting savings are quantifiable and comparable to the affiliate's service fee
claims.mechanism: When the CFO service demonstrably saves a client more than it costs, presenting that math to the client reframes the affiliate's fee as effectively zero; this lowers perceived risk for the end client and makes the affiliate appear to deliver more value than they charge.
bounded_by: Requires the accounting savings to be large enough to offset the affiliate's service fee in the client's P&L

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends cold-acquiring new e-commerce agency affiliates via paid ads directly targeting B2B agencies, rather than relying solely on warm network affiliates, because agencies have a predictable roster of clients (e.g., 30 clients each) that makes a single agency relationship worth a concentrated batch of referrals in a short time.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: E-commerce accounting/CFO SaaS targeting e-commerce brands
Affiliate program offering per-deal commissions at $6K+ per client

anchors.region: example agency with 30 clients at 50% webinar conversion
commission earned by that agency in first month at $6K/deal

claims.text: Alex Hormozi argues that for a high-authority B2B service like accounting and CFO advisory, content-driven ads built from real client interactions (logos, documented savings, tactical hooks showing what generic bookkeepers miss) outperform direct-response product ads because prospects must trust an authority figure before buying.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: B2B professional service requiring high trust and perceived expertise (e.g., CFO, accounting, legal)
Cold audience unfamiliar with the brand

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends an organic-content-to-ad-funnel sequence for an authority-based B2B service: post high volume of tactical content demonstrating client results, connect ManyChat to generate inbound leads from organic posts, route them into a VSL-to-close funnel, then identify the best-performing organic pieces and redistribute them as paid ads into the same funnel.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: B2B professional service where authority demonstration is the primary conversion lever
Founder or brand has demonstrable client results to document
bounded_by: Hormozi recommends affiliates first, then the ad funnel; the ad funnel is a secondary channel, not a replacement

claims.text: Alex Hormozi asserts that the LTV-to-CAC ratio should be at least 3:1 before committing to a commission payout level, and that a business generating $36,000-$60,000 gross profit per customer (at 55-60% gross margin on ~$100K LTV) can sustain $6,000-$10,000 affiliate commissions while maintaining a favorable ratio.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: B2B SaaS or subscription service with near-zero churn
Gross margins 55-60%
LTV in the $50,000-$100,000+ range
claims.mechanism: The 3:1 gross-profit-to-CAC floor ensures that even after paying the affiliate, the business captures at least 2x its acquisition cost in gross profit on the first customer.

numbers.axis: gross profit per customer vs. affiliate commission at 55-60% gross margin

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommends continuing to scale the affiliate channel before opening a paid ad funnel, framing the current affiliate program as being six inches from a breakthrough that would provide a low-cost, compounding lead source; adding the ad channel before exhausting affiliate potential dilutes focus without payoff.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Affiliate channel is active but not yet fully saturated
Commission level has not yet been raised to the recommended range
Activation friction has not yet been removed
bounded_by: Hormozi does endorse adding an ad funnel eventually once the affiliate base is established

examples.text: Hormozi illustrates the commission motivating-threshold claim with an extreme comparison: if you offer an affiliate $10, it is not motivating; if you offer a million dollars, it is. The argument is that $2,000 falls below the threshold where the financial reward overrides the activation friction, while $6,000-$10,000 crosses it for a business owner whose time is valuable.

Hormozi uses a concrete agency math example to justify cold-acquiring agency affiliates via ads: an e-commerce agency with 30 clients that does a webinar and converts half would refer 15 clients. At $6,000 per closed deal, that agency earns $90,000 in a single month. Hormozi notes this creates a powerful affiliate testimonial that can itself be used to recruit further affiliates.

To illustrate the ease-of-activation lever, Hormozi describes the full handholding package: pre-written emails (built with AI assistance), a custom-branded landing page, and sending a team member to plug everything into the affiliate's CRM. The framing is that the affiliate has zero tasks remaining after agreeing to the program.

practices.text: Present two commission tiers explicitly (e.g., $2K standard vs. $3K-$10K bonus) and tie the higher tier to a 30-day activation window so affiliates face a clear, time-bounded choice.

When white-labeling a service through an affiliate, prepare a client-facing ROI calculation showing that the service saves more than it costs so the affiliate can position it as free to their clients.
Post a high volume of tactical, client-result content on organic channels and connect ManyChat to capture inbound leads; promote the best-performing pieces as paid ads into a VSL-to-close funnel.

To recruit cold agency affiliates at scale, run targeted B2B ads to e-commerce agencies, then get them on the phone and pitch the commission program as a revenue-only opportunity with no cost to the agency.
